  "findings": [
    "Aluminum branch circuit conductors terminated on a wiring device not marked or rated CO/ALR",
    "Fire hazard due to galvanic corrosion and thermal expansion mismatch between aluminum wire and standard brass/steel terminals"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Replace the non-compliant wiring device with one specifically rated and marked CO/ALR",
    "Alternatively, pig-tail the aluminum wire to a copper conductor using listed Al/Cu wire connectors before terminating on the standard device"
  ],
  "rationale": "Terminating solid aluminum branch-circuit conductors (typically 15A or 20A) on standard wiring devices not rated CO/ALR is a severe code violation and fire hazard. Standard terminals do not account for the thermal expansion and galvanic properties of aluminum, leading to loose connections and overheating.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 406.3(C)",
    "NEC 110.14",
    "NEC 310"
  ]
}
